title: Advanced parallel "many-core" framework for reservoir simulation
abstract: The disclosure relates generally to a parallel computing framework designed to accelerate intensive kernel operations and address the complex physics in numerical reservoir simulations by effectively using the 'many-core' computing platform. Specifically, this Many-core Advanced Parallel Simulation (MAPS) uses heuristics to decide the optimal hardware configurations and optimal software components for a given problem's characteristics to efficiently design a model.
claims:
Claims 1. An apparatus for performing a simulation of a subsurface hydrocarbon reservoir, comprising: a) a database configured to store data comprising equations that represent the thermodynamic calculations associated with black-oil, compositional and thermal flow scenarios, and field measurements of said subsurface hydrocarbon reservoir being simulated; b) a plurality of data processors, including at least one central processing unit and at least one graphical processing unit, configured to receive the stored data from the database and to execute software components; c) an integrated set of software components, capable of massive parallel computations of said stored data, executable on said plurality of data processors; d) a software program executable by said plurality of data processors, the software program containing a computer readable software instruction, comprising: i) a set of heuristic rules configured to apply a best guess estimation to the stored data to determine the appropriation hardware and software configuration to efficiently simulate said hydrocarbon reservoir; and ii) an adaptive strategy to choose successive hardware configurations as the simulation progresses; e) a memory for storing the results obtained by execution of said software components by said plurality of data processors on said stored data; and f) an output display for displaying results obtained by execution of software components by said plurality of data processors on said stored data. 2. The apparatus of claim 0, wherein said software configuration is at least one of solvers, thermodynamic calculations, adaptive modeling, and reduce modeling component. 3. The apparatus of claim 0, wherein said hardware configuration is at least one of said central processing unit and at least of said graphical processing unit. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein said hardware configuration is a 1 :10 ratio of said central processing units to said graphical processing units. 5. The apparatus of claim 0, wherein said field measurements is at least one of fluid pressure, fluid saturation, multiple-phase fluid flow, reservoir porosity and lithology distribution, permeability distribution, and water saturation. 6. A method of performing a simulation of a subsurface hydrocarbon reservoir, the reservoir being approximated by a reservoir model having a plurality of blocks, each block having associated therewith an equation set representing a reservoir property, the method comprising: a) using a heuristic method to decide on the best hardware and software configuration for solving a system of equations formed using the equation set for each cell in the plurality of blocks; b) applying said hardware and said software configuration to said system of equations; and c) solving said system of equations in parallel. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein said reservoir property is at least one of fluid pressure, fluid saturation, multiple-phase fluid flow, reservoir porosity and lithology distribution, permeability distribution, and water saturation. 8. The method of claim 6, wherein said hardware configuration is a plurality of CPUs and GPUs. 9. The method of claim 6, wherein said software configuration is at least one of solvers, thermodynamic calculations, adaptive modeling, and reduce modeling component. 10. A method of simulation of a reservoir model, comprising: a) generating a reservoir geological model from measured reservoir properties, wherein said reservoir model having a plurality of blocks, each block having associated therewith an equation 

 set representing said reservoir property; b) pre-processing the reservoir model by choosing a partitioning scheme using heuristic rules, said heuristic rules partitioning the blocks into a plurality of processing elements to be solved using a combination of serial and parallel software components and hardware components; c) partitioning the reservoir model into a plurality of processing elements using said partitioning scheme; d) solving said equation set using a combination of said parallel and serial software components; e) post-processing the partitioned reservoir model to further refine the parallel and serial performance of the partitioned calculation; f) re-partitioning the generated reservoir model into a plurality of domains dynamically to improve parallel and serial performance; and g) repeating steps d-f using the dynamically modified partitioning scheme and parameters. 11. A method for simulating a subsurface reservoir using a computer framework, wherein said computer framework has a hybrid hardware processor and software components, the method comprising: a) uploading reservoir measurements; b) selecting desired physics model; c) applying a set of heuristic rules to dynamically decide on a configuration of said hardware and said software components for the specific parameters of the said physics model and reservoir measurements; d) applying chosen hardware and software configuration to said reservoir measurements; and 

 e) solving said physics model. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein said desired physics model is selected from black-oil, compositional, and thermal models. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein said software components are selected from linear solver, non-linear solver, thermodynamic calculations, adaptive modeling strategies, and reductive model strategies. 14. The method of claim 11 , wherein said hybrid hardware processor is a many-core processor with multiple CPU and GPU cores. 15. A method for simulating a subsurface reservoir using a computer framework, wherein said computer framework has a hybrid hardware processor and software components, the method comprising: a) uploading geological reservoir measurements; b) creating a static reservoir model of the field using said geological reservoir measurements, wherein said reservoir model is composed of a series of interconnected blocks that are assigned physical properties of the reservoir; c) selecting desired physics model to simulate flow through said static reservoir model; d) assigning equations to said series of interconnected blocks to represent said physics model; e) applying a set of heuristic rules to determine a configuration of said hardware and said software components for solving the specific parameters of the said physics model and reservoir measurements; f) applying chosen hardware and software configuration to said reservoir measurements; g) simulating said physics model by solving said equations; h) repeating steps d-f as simulation progresses; and 

 i) displaying said simulation on a display for review. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein said software components are selected from linear solver, non-linear solver, thermodynamic calculations, adaptive modeling strategies, and reductive model strategies. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein said hybrid hardware processor is a many-core processor with multiple CPU and GPU cores.
